content.text:
You are resolving a real issue in the facebook/zstd repository (c).

## Issue
Cap hashlog for row based matchfinder, chainlog for short cache matchfinders

[This assert](https://github.com/embg/zstd/blob/dev/lib/compress/zstd_compress_internal.h#L785) which was added as part of short cache has uncovered two bugs:
* The short cache PR only validates that hashLog is <= 24 bits. We need to do the same for chainLog, this has showed up in some CI failures.
* The row based matchfinder needs to have hashLog capped at 28 bits (24 + 4). The assert linked above can currently be triggered by `./zstd -o /dev/null -7 < ~/silesia.tar --zstd=hlog=29`

These bugs can't lead to data corruption, but they do have some bad effects:
* Can regress compression ratio by corrupting the hashtable
* Every time the assert fails, that is undefined behavior (shift by larger than width of type)
* Cause CI failures for debug builds

Code pointer: https://github.com/facebook/zstd/blob/4f7183d887789d4d2bb2e5af850c427f1df725ff/lib/compress/zstd_compress.c#L1443

## Task
Investigate the codebase and make the minimal source changes needed to resolve the issue.
Do NOT modify, add, or delete any test files — the grader applies its own tests.
Edit the code in place. When you are confident the fix is complete, stop.

Implemented minimal source fix in `lib/compress/zstd_compress.c`.

Changes made:
- Added a row-matchfinder hashLog cap at `28` (`32 - 8 tag bits + min rowLog 4`).
- Applied the row cap when resolving/using row matchfinder params, including context reset safety.
- Extended short-cache CDict parameter adjustment to cap both:
  - `hashLog`
  - `chainLog`
  at `32 - ZSTD_SHORT_CACHE_TAG_BITS`.

Verification:
- Built successfully with `make -j2`.
- Ran targeted reproduction successfully:
  - `programs/zstd -q -o /dev/null -7 --zstd=hlog=29` on bounded input.
